refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to maintain solution-neutral safety focus without specifying latency values or technology. The FR is expressed in the problem domain (what) without how to achieve it.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed bandwidth and quality of service
Implement local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Communication protocol tightly coupled with system architecture
Predictive control algorithms dependent on latency characteristics
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which can be implemented independently of specific communication protocols or predictive algorithms, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency occasionally exceeds thresholds, adding complexity
Dedicated communication channels may not be feasible in all environments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive control strategies or communication quality management as secondary DPs to handle variability in network conditions and ensure consistent latency performance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams received and sent for processing and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems and components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is the core system handling data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module involves hardware and control but less focused on data processing; would require significant diffusion to handle real-time processing.
Teleoperation involves remote control and may process some data, but real-time processing core is broader than this module.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not primary real-time data processing; high diffusion if chosen.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing a system architecture prioritizing real-time data processing and minimal buffering, as it centralizes core processing functions and interfaces directly with telemetry and network links.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if some real-time processing is distributed there.
Risk of underestimating buffering needs in Vehicle or Safety Services modules leading to cross-module dependencies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment proves weak, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers, allowing clearer module boundaries between Vehicle (data acquisition) and NGHE Solution (processing).

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the required teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if some real-time processing is distributed there.
Risk of underestimating buffering needs in Vehicle or Safety Services modules leading to cross-module dependencies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate encapsulation of the DP. It interfaces directly with Telemetry and Network Link, supporting centralized real-time processing. However, some diffusion risk exists if real-time processing responsibilities are spread to Teleoperation or Vehicle modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end communication latency within the NGHE Solution module and its interfaces, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and well-justified, with the module encompassing core real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies necessary to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: If containment proves weak, refine the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers, allowing clearer module boundaries between Vehicle (data acquisition) and NGHE Solution (processing).

Monitor and manage potential diffusion risks by clearly defining interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle modules to avoid cross-module buffering and processing overlaps.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows strong cohesion and appropriate encapsulation with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level and focus on monitoring and managing identified diffusion risks rather than further decomposition at this stage.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain its high containment and low diffusion scores during implementation.
Interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle modules will be clearly defined and enforced.
open_risks: Potential overlap of real-time processing responsibilities across modules leading to diffusion risks.
Underestimation of buffering needs in Vehicle or Safety Services modules causing cross-module dependencies.
evidence_needed: Empirical measurements of end-to-end communication latency within NGHE Solution and its interfaces under operational conditions.
Verification reports confirming p95 latency ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions.
